name: SST (Serverless Stack Toolkit)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); pay for underlying AWS/Cloudflare resources only

auth.notes: Uses AWS/Cloudflare credentials from local environment

performance.notes: Framework overhead minimal; performance determined by deployed functions

agent_readiness.notes: TypeScript framework for building full-stack applications on AWS and Cloudflare. Infrastructure as TypeScript code with type safety. Live Lambda development (hot reload). Component model: API, Function, SSTv3 uses Pulumi internally. Automatic resource linking with type-safe resource access. Database, auth, jobs, queues as typed components. Popular for Next.js + AWS serverless apps. Active community.

gotchas: SST v2 to v3 breaking migration — different mental model
AWS ecosystem lock-in (though Cloudflare support added)
Local development with Live Lambda requires internet connection
Pulumi state management added complexity in v3
best_when: TypeScript full-stack apps on AWS wanting type-safe infrastructure
Next.js on AWS with serverless functions
Teams wanting CDK-like IaC with better developer experience
Rapid prototyping of serverless apps with hot reload
summary: SST is a TypeScript framework for building full-stack serverless applications on AWS and Cloudflare. Infrastructure as TypeScript code with type safety. Hot reload via Live Lambda. Type-safe resource linking (no string ARNs). Popular for Next.js on AWS. v3 uses Pulumi internally. Best for TypeScript teams wanting great developer experience for AWS serverless apps.
